---
format:
  html:
    css: assets/css/custom.css
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    title-block-banner: false
---

::: {.hero-shell}
::: {.hero-image role="img" aria-label="Placeholder for Everglades photograph"}
:::

::: {.hero-copy .container}
# Thinking Across Scales

How studying landscapes became a way of understanding the world.
:::
:::

::: {.section .intro-shell .container}
::: {.intro-copy .text-center .mx-auto}
I was trained as a landscape ecologist, where the most important lesson was not about landscapes themselves, but about learning to think across scales. Over time, that perspective expanded beyond ecology into computation, organizations, design, and everyday life. This website is an evolving record of that journey.
:::
:::

::: {.section .container-fluid .feature-cards-shell}
::: {.row .gx-4 .gy-4}
::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
⟲
:::

### Thinking Across Scales

Ideas about feedback, hierarchy, dynamics, and systems.

[Explore →](#)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
📍
:::

### Journeys

Places that transformed how I think.

[Explore →](#)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
⌘
:::

### Computational Thinking

Modern scientific workflows and digital craftsmanship.

[Explore →](#)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
✍️
:::

### Writing

Essays, notes, and ongoing reflections.

[Explore →](#)
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .container-fluid .highlight-shell}
::: {.row .gx-4 .gy-4}
::: {.col-12 .col-md-6}
::: {.highlight-panel}
::: {.panel-image}
:::

::: {.panel-copy}
Featured Essay

### Why Scale Matters

An exploration of scale as a guiding principle for science, design, and systems thinking.

[Read More →](#)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6}
::: {.highlight-panel}
::: {.panel-image}
:::

::: {.panel-copy}
Latest Journey

### Everglades

A field story about water, fire, networks, and the scales where landscape processes become visible.

[View Journey →](#)
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .quote-shell}
> “I am not interested in whether two systems look alike. I am interested in whether they are organized by the same principles.”
:::

::: {.footer-shell .container}
::: {.footer-row}
Thinking Across Scales

Built with Quarto

Hosted on GitHub
:::
:::
